task_completion.description: The agent successfully searches for and opens the chart for patient Jayson Fadel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR interface displays 'Jayson Fadel' in the patient header or active patient frame.

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the Transactions section and initiates a new Referral.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the 'Transactions' list or the 'Add Transaction' form with 'Referral' selected as the type.

task_completion.description: The agent specifies Cardiology or Cardiologist as the referral destination.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Referral form, the 'Refer To' field contains 'Cardiology' or 'Cardiologist'.

task_completion.description: The agent documents Hypertension as the reason for the referral.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Referral form, the 'Diagnosis' or 'Reason' field contains 'Hypertension', 'HTN', or 'Blood pressure'.

task_completion.description: The referral is successfully saved and appears in the patient's transaction history.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A list of transactions is visible showing a saved 'Referral' entry with today's date, or a summary view of the saved referral details.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the OpenEMR web application via a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser window displaying the OpenEMR user interface.

integrity.description: The agent does not use terminal commands or SQL injection to insert the referral directly into the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots do not show a terminal, command prompt, or database administration tool being used to input data.

integrity.description: The agent uses standard GUI elements (buttons, forms) rather than pasting pre-computed API requests.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent filling out form fields or clicking 'Save' buttons within the OpenEMR interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Jayson Fadel. Target Specialist: Cardiology or Cardiologist. Condition/Reason: Hypertension or HTN.
